title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on the historical and philosophical dynamics of forgiveness, exploring how forgiveness functioned as a social mechanism that could entrap or constrain actors. Palmer examines forgiveness not as purely ethical but as a complex social and historical phenomenon.
writeup: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer on forgiveness as a historical and philosophical phenomenon rather than a purely ethical one. The conversation examines how forgiveness operated as a social mechanism that could entrap or constrain the actors involved. The framing matters because it treats a concept usually read as moral as a tool embedded in social and historical dynamics, where extending or accepting forgiveness carried strategic consequences.
